title: CatalogRecord
description: A record in a catalog, describing the registration of a single resource

status.title: change type
status.description: The status of the catalog record in the context of editorial flow of the dataset and data service descriptions

conformsTo.title: application profile
conformsTo.description: An Application Profile that the Catalog Record's metadata conforms to

description.title: Descriptions
description.description: A list of free-text accounts of the catalog record
examples: This catalog record describes the registration of the Climate Data 2023 dataset.
Contains metadata about when the dataset was added and last updated.

issued.title: listing date
issued.description: List of dates on which the catalog record was included in the catalog

anyOf.description: A year and month in YYYY-MM format

_oldDocs.range: rdfs:Literal (typed as xsd:date, xsd:dateTime, xsd:gYear or xsd:gYearMonth)

language.title: language
language.description: Language code used in catalog record metadata text, using ISO 639-1 values such as en or es, full list can be seen at https://id.loc.gov/vocabulary/iso639-1.html

modified.title: update/modification date
modified.description: The most recent date on which the catalog record was changed or modified

anyOf.description: A year and month in YYYY-MM format

_oldDocs.range: rdfs:Literal (typed as xsd:date, xsd:dateTime, xsd:gYear or xsd:gYearMonth)

source.title: source metadata
source.description: The original metadata that was used in creating metadata for the items in the catalog record, either a URL referencing the source metadata or a string of the source metadata itself
examples: Original metadata harvested from NOAA data portal

title.title: title
title.description: A name given to the Catalog Record

primaryTopic.title: primary topic
primaryTopic.description: A link to the Dataset, Data service or Catalog described in the Catalog Record

_oldDocs.rationale: While its use is not mandatory, the incorporation of dcat:CatalogRecord into DCAT-US 3.0 holds significant value. It enables catalogs to distinguish between metadata describing datasets or services and the actual catalog entries. This differentiation proves especially advantageous for ensuring adherence to application profiles that demand specific metadata for catalog records. Furthermore, it streamlines resource lifecycle management, empowering catalogs to monitor alterations and revisions to their entries, ultimately bolstering data governance and quality assurance protocols.
